data.title: Coinbase stock gains after SEC poised to dismiss suit
data.description:
Crypto exchange Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN) stock climbed 2.4% in early Friday trading after its CEO, Brian Armstrong, said the company reached an agreement with the Securities and Exchange Commission's staff to dismiss its litigation against Coinbase.

He said the SEC indicated the dismissal would be announced next week. "This would be a full dismissal, with $0 in fines paid and zero changes to our business," Armstrong said in a post on X.

"This is hugely vindicating, especially because many people questioned my decision to engage in litigation with the SEC on this matter in 2023," he said.

In 2023, the SEC filed the suit, alleging that the company was operating as an unregistered securities broker. The SEC, under the Biden administration had contended that most crypto tokens qualify as securities contracts, whereas Coinbase (NASDAQ:COIN) asserts that crypto assets are more like commodities.

"I have to give credit here to the Trump administration, for winning the election, and for the departure of the activist head of the SEC, Gary Gensler," Armstrong noted.

He also contended that the lawsuit cost taxpayers millions of dollars and has done "irreparable harm" to the country.

Going forward, he expects to "continue working productively with the SEC on any number of items over the years, just as we do with every agency around the world where we operate," Armstrong wrote.

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Time is running out for TrueCar to reach profitability - J.P. Morgan
data.description:
TrueCar’s (NASDAQ:TRUE) disappointing fourth quarter results coupled with uncertainty surrounding its efforts to boost growth and profitability leads J.P. Morgan to move to the sidelines, downgrading the stock to Neutral from Overweight and withdrawing its price target.

“We continue to see execution risk with TRUE navigating multiple transitions, including building out a new digital consumer experience, growing its original equipment manufacturers/Trade businesses, and filling the gap left by the loss of the USAA partnership with Navy Federal Credit Union,” J.P. Morgan analyst team led by Rajat Gupta said in Friday’s note to clients.

TrueCar’s (NASDAQ:TRUE) Q4 results were a miss on both revenue and EBITDA, with softer results on both the dealer marketplace and OEM initiatives. The disappointing results also came with a downbeat outlook into Q1 that included revenue growth of high-single-digits, and an EBITDA loss of ~$5M (versus the consensus for a loss of $1M).

“We believe the clock is clearly ticking for TRUE to showcase fruits of its initiatives and upcoming investments in sales and marketing,” Gupta and team said. “As such, given the diminishing cycle support from inventories and lack of visibility on sustained inflection in revenue/profits, we move to Neutral from Overweight.”

Accordingly, J.P. Morgan lowers its FY25 EBITDA estimate to breakeven from +$15M prior, and FY26 EBITDA estimate to +$25M vs +$35M prior. Regarding EPS, Gupta et al., now expects TrueCar (NASDAQ:TRUE) to report a loss of $0.24 per share in FY25 from initial guidance for a loss of $0.05 per share. In FY26, J.P. Morgan lowers its estimate to a loss of $0.07 from a profit of $0.17.

data.title: Equinor plans sale of Argentina onshore assets - report
data.description:
Equinor (NYSE:EQNR) is looking to sell its onshore assets in Argentina's Vaca Muerta region, Reuters reported Friday, citing the Mas Energia online publication.

The company reportedly wants to sell its 30% stake in the Bandurria Sur licence and 50% in Bajo del Toro Norte, and does not plan to sell its stakes in Argentina offshore licences, where Vaca Muerta holds the world's second-largest shale gas reserves and fourth-largest shale oil reserves.

Argentina's YPF (YPF), which has right of first refusal, holds a 40% stake in Bandurria Sur and 50% in Bajo del Toro Norte, while Shell owns a 30% stake in Bandurria Sur; Equinor's (NYSE:EQNR) stakes in the two licences could be valued at ~$1.3B, Jefferies analysts said, citing Wood Mackenzie estimates.

Bandurria Sur produces ~70K boe/day while Bajo del Toro Norte has yet to be developed.

data.title: UnitedHealth Group shares dive, sending ripple effects to the ETF market
data.description:
UnitedHealth Group's (NYSE:UNH) stock saw a significant decline on Friday morning following a report from The Wall Street Journal revealing that the U.S. Department of Justice (DoJ) has initiated a new investigation into the company's business practices.

As a result, shares of UnitedHealth Group (NYSE:UNH) have fallen by 9.4%, and this decline has also led to a pullback in several exchange traded funds that hold significant portfolio weightings in the multinational healthcare giant.

Outlined below are the 10 largest exchange traded fund holders of UNH, all which trade lower on Friday:

No. 10: iShares Global Healthcare ETF (NYSEARCA:IXJ), 6.26% weighting in UNH.

No. 9: SPDR Dow Jones Industrial Average ETF Trust (NYSEARCA:DIA), 6.90% weighting in UNH.

No. 8: Global X Dow 30 Covered Call & Growth ETF (NYSEARCA:DYLG), 6.95% weighting in UNH.

No. 7: Global X Dow 30 Covered Call ETF (NYSEARCA:DJIA), 6.99% weighting in UNH.

No. 6: JPMorgan Healthcare Leaders ETF (NASDAQ:JDOC), 7.54% weighting in UNH.

No. 5: Fidelity MSCI Health Care Index ETF (NYSEARCA:FHLC), 7.57% weighting in UNH.

No. 4: Vanguard Health Care ETF (NYSEARCA:VHT), 8.05% weighting in UNH.

No. 3: iShares U.S. Healthcare ETF (NYSEARCA:IYH), 8.51% weighting in UNH.

No. 2: Health Care Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLV), 8.73% weighting in UNH.

No. 1: iShares U.S. Healthcare Providers ETF (NYSEARCA:IHF), 20.77% weighting in UNH.

Year-to-date price action: UNH -9.9%, IXJ +6.6%, DIA +2.9%, DYLG +3.7%, DJIA +3%, JDOC +7.4%, FHLC +5.9%, VHT +5.8%, IYH +5.4%, XLV +6.1% and IHF +5%.

data.title: UnitedHealth falls after report on new DoJ probe
data.description:
Shares of UnitedHealth Group (NYSE:UNH) dropped ~7% in the premarket on Friday after The Wall Street Journal reported that the U.S. Department of Justice ((DoJ)) has launched a new investigation into the company’s business practices.

Citing people familiar with the matter, the publication said that the DoJ’s civil fraud investigation relates to how UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH) adds questionable diagnoses to its members’ medical records to benefit from extra payments to its Medicare Advantage plans.

The probe also involves the Office of Inspector General at the U.S. Department of Health and Human Services, one of the people said.

UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH) declined to comment. Spokespersons for DoJ and Health Department’s Office of Inspector General also refused to comment.

However, in a statement on its website, UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH) later criticized the Journal over its reporting. “The Wall Street Journal continues to report misinformation on the Medicare Advantage (MA) program, the company said, adding, “Any suggestion that our practices are fraudulent is outrageous and false.”

The Minnetonka, Minnesota-based managed care giant has been the subject of several DOJ civil investigations in recent months. In February 2024, the agency launched an antitrust investigation into UnitedHealth (NYSE:UNH), and in November, the DoJ sued to block the company’s plans to acquire the home-health company Amedisys (AMED) for about $3.3B.

Update 10.11 AM EST: Adds UNH statement

data.title: Biggest stock movers Friday: UNH, MELI, CELH, XYZ, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged lower on Friday as investors digested the rise in initial jobless claims, signaling a modest softening in the labor market. Investors eagerly awaited the consumer sentiment report for further insights into economic conditions and consumer confidence.

Here are some of Friday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • MercadoLibre (NASDAQ:MELI) shares surged 12% after delivering strong Q4 results that exceeded expectations across key metrics, including active buyers, gross merchandise volume (GMV), and payment volume, driving a 37.4% Y/Y increase in total sales. Revenue of $6.1B beat estimates by $120M, fueled by a 33% rise in total payment volume and an 8% increase in GMV. EPS soared to $12.61, nearly quadrupling from the prior year. Brazil, its largest market, saw a 13% GMV increase and a 38% jump in consolidated revenue and financial income.
  • Celsius Holdings (NASDAQ:CELH) jumped 18% after confirming its $1.8B acquisition of rival energy drink brand Alani Nu alongside strong Q4 earnings. The deal, expected to close in Q2, includes $1.65B in price and $150M in tax assets, with $900M in debt financing and $375M in cash on hand. Celsius expects the acquisition to drive topline growth, be cash EPS accretive in year one, and strengthen its position in the global energy drink market, which is projected to grow at a 10% CAGR through 2029. The company also anticipates $50M in run-rate cost synergies over two years.

Biggest stock losers

  • Block (NYSE:XYZ) shares tumbled nearly 7% after missing Q4 earnings estimates and issuing a disappointing outlook for Q1 and FY2025. The company guided Q1 gross profit at $2.32B, below the $2.38B Visible Alpha consensus, and expects adjusted operating income of $430M, up from $402M in Q4 2024. For FY2025, Block forecasted gross profit of $10.22B, trailing the $10.27B consensus but rising from $8.89B in 2024, with expectations for Cash App and Square to improve in the latter half of the year. It reaffirmed its goal to achieve Rule of 40 in 2026 and exit 2025 at or above that threshold on a quarterly basis. CEO Jack Dorsey highlighted plans to integrate its ecosystems, expand sales efforts, and invest in AI tools, while noting increased marketing spending in H2 2024 yielded strong returns.
  • IAMGOLD (NYSE:IAG) shares plunged as much as 9% after falling short of expectations in Q4 results, despite a ~58% Y/Y revenue increase. Looking ahead, the company expects total attributable production in 2025 between 735,000 and 820,000 ounces, up from 2024, driven by Côté Gold’s ramp-up and steady output from Essakane and Westwood. Côté Gold’s first full-year production is projected at 360,000–400,000 ounces (250,000–280,000 ounces attributable). Cash costs are forecasted at $1,200–$1,350 per ounce, slightly higher due to increased costs at Essakane. Capital expenditures are set at $310M, with $290M allocated for sustaining capital.
  • Nu Holdings (NYSE:NU) shares slumped 9% after reporting Q4 earnings and revenue that missed estimates, as purchase volume declined sequentially to $31.1B from $32.6B in Q3 but rose from $23.3B in Q4 2023. The Brazilian digital bank added 5.5M new customers, bringing its total to 99.3M. Monthly average revenue per active customer increased to $11.4 from $10.6 in Q3 and $8.6 a year ago, while the cost to serve per customer remained steady at $0.9, up from $0.8 in Q4 2023.
  • Akamai Technologies (NASDAQ:AKAM) plummeted 9% despite strong Q4 results, as its 2025 guidance fell short of expectations. The company projected adjusted EPS of $6.00–$6.40, missing the $6.82 consensus, and revenue of $4.0B–$4.2B, with a midpoint of $4.1B below the $4.25B consensus. It also forecast an adjusted operating margin of 28%, trailing expectations. For Q1, Akamai expects adjusted EPS of $1.54–$1.59, below the $1.61 consensus, and revenue of $1.0B–$1.02B, missing the $1.04B consensus.
  • UnitedHealth Group (NYSE:UNH) shares lost ~11% following a report that the U.S. Department of Justice ((DoJ)) has launched a civil fraud investigation into the company’s business practices. The probe, which also reportedly involves the Office of Inspector General at the Department of Health and Human Services, focuses on allegations that UnitedHealth inflated patient diagnoses to secure higher Medicare Advantage payments. This adds to the company’s ongoing regulatory challenges, including a DoJ antitrust investigation in February and a lawsuit in November to block its $3.3B acquisition of Amedisys (AMED), raising concerns over its business operations and future growth.
